H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere:
Pebble (H-OS-L1) achieved n=95, mean 7.15 (std=1.78) with strong confidence.
• Right‑skewed distribution peaks at score 8, with 66% positive responses.
• Qualitative n=29 highlighted noticeable improvement and loved music; YouTube views are 30, the highest in HEALING, though retention data is missing.
• AutoGluon predicts a score of 6.86, slightly lower than observed, indicating real‑world performance exceeds model expectations.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble’s calming rhythm helped overstimulated listeners relax, with many loving the music and high views.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere:
Rainfall (H-OS-L2) recorded n=51, mean 6.92 (std=1.73) with strong confidence.
• Distribution clusters at scores 7–8, with 65% positive responses.
• Qualitative n=21 noted noticeable improvement and very settling rhythm; YouTube views are 5, low reach.
• AutoGluon predictions align closely with observed mean.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall gave noticeable improvement to most, with a very settling rhythm and modest reach.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere:

• Qualitative n=23 cited noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm; YouTube views are 8.
• AutoGluon predictions match observed outcomes.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier offered balanced calm for overstimulation, with positive responses and modest reach.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere:
The Meadow protocol (H-AX-L1) recorded n=67 listeners with a mean outcome score of 7.22 (std=1.93) and a strong confidence tier.
• Distribution is right‑skewed, with 63% positive responses indicating majority experienced at least moderate improvement.
• Qualitative data from n=23 respondents highlighted noticeable to significant improvement and a very settling rhythm; YouTube shows only 7 cumulative views, suggesting limited discovery.
• AutoGluon predicts a score of 7.84, exceeding the observed mean by roughly 0.6, flagging potential under‑exposure.
• Listeners typically settled within 5–10 minutes and reported liking the music.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners felt calmer and more grounded after using H-AX-L1, with most settling within 10 minutes.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere:
Cottage (H-AX-L2) achieved n=48 with a mean of 6.94 (std=1.62) and a meaningful confidence tier.
• Scores cluster around 7, with 67% positive responses, indicating noticeable improvement for most listeners.
• Qualitative n=20 feedback noted mixed music opinions and neutral to liked rhythm; YouTube views are 6, indicating low reach.
• AutoGluon does not list this protocol among top predictions, suggesting room for efficacy growth.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: H-AX-L2 helped users feel more centered, though music opinions varied and views are low.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley

• AutoGluon predicts a score aligned with observed mean, offering no discrepancy.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: H-AX-L3 offered soothing music that many found settling, though a few reported no effect.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The current run shows overall efficacy across the Drum Protocols with modest variance, yet several protocols display under‑exploited potential.
• HEALING protocols exhibit the strongest consistency and highest positive response rates.
• THRIVING protocols achieve elevated means, especially in Focus & Clarity variants.
• TRANSFORMING protocols display the widest spread, reflecting their ascending‑arc design.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series each reveal distinct patterns aligned with their design intent.
• HEALING shows the lowest variance and tight clustering of outcomes.
• THRIVING displays the highest means, particularly in Focus & Clarity HOLD protocols.
• TRANSFORMING carries the most dispersion, consistent with its ascending‑arc architecture.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols deviate from series‑wide trends, suggesting targeted refinement opportunities.
• X-MC-L3 exhibits a low mean (5.7) and high variance (2.45), indicating possible bimodality.
• Several crisis DESCENT protocols (H-CR-L1/L2/L3) score lower than other HEALING entries, reflecting expected crisis floor but still meaningful.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted iterations could amplify under‑recognized efficacy and address emerging signals.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 (n=10) low mean 5.7, high std 2.45 – warrants redesign or L2 variant.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 (n=95) strong signal – suggests amplifying exposure to increase reach.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 (n=35) high mean 7.63 – candidate for L2 expansion.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MD-L3 (n=13) high mean 7.15, wide std – needs larger sample confirmation.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding diagnostics reveal both stability and Evolution in analytical framing.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 24 of 30 protocols show similarity <0.85; X-MD-L3 drops to 0.507, indicating notable drift.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: HEALING AX family tightly linked (~0.89 similarity); H-CR-L1 most isolated (mean sim 0.575).
• Confidence: Drift detection is reliable; fine‑grained stability remains limited by run count.
